# Consistency Checklist: Cztery Sensory Optymalnych Okien Sprzedazy Energii

**Purpose**: Validate that the feature requirements remain consistent across the spec, plan, and tasks, with emphasis on scope boundaries, traceability, and review readiness.
**Created**: 2026-05-12
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [x] CHK001 Are all four sensor variants still explicitly defined by both day and time slice across the current spec, plan summary, and task breakdown? [Completeness, Spec §FR-001]
- [x] CHK002 Does the documentation set clearly preserve additive scope, so the four new sensors are added beside existing sensors rather than framed as replacements anywhere? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001, Spec §FR-001a]
- [x] CHK003 Is the explicit exclusion of buy-window sensors preserved consistently across the active feature documents, rather than only in one clarification entry? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001b]
- [x] CHK004 Are the required sensor outputs fully documented as state plus ranked attributes, without leaving any artifact to infer missing output fields from another document? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-012]

## Requirement Clarity

- [x] CHK005 Is the term "one-hour window" used consistently enough that no artifact could be read as reviving the older quarter-hour midday model for these new sensors? [Clarity, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005, Conflict]
- [x] CHK006 Is the ranking rule stated clearly enough that "best" and "second-best" always mean first and second in one deterministic global order for a slice? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011]
- [x] CHK007 Is the availability rule precise enough that reviewers can distinguish "no valid result" from "partial but tempting result" without relying on implementation knowledge? [Clarity, Spec §FR-014]
- [x] CHK008 Is the state contract unambiguous that sensors publish only the best-window start in `HH:MM`, not a range string or date-qualified timestamp? [Clarity, Spec §FR-007]

## Cross-Artifact Consistency

- [x] CHK009 Does the plan keep the feature centered on sell-price payloads only, without introducing buy-price semantics that would conflict with the spec scope? [Consistency, Spec §FR-002, Spec §FR-001b]
- [x] CHK010 Do the plan constraints and task descriptions preserve the same morning and evening time ranges defined in the spec, with no alternate boundaries implied elsewhere? [Consistency, Spec §FR-004, Spec §FR-005]
- [x] CHK011 Are full-hour input semantics and full-hour candidate starts described consistently across spec, plan, and tasks, rather than mixing payload granularity with candidate granularity? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003a, Spec §FR-005a]
- [x] CHK012 Do the tasks maintain the same additive coexistence goal as the spec by treating preservation of existing midday sensors as prerequisite work rather than incidental cleanup? [Consistency, Spec §FR-001a, Traceability]
- [x] CHK013 Is omission of `second_window_gap_pct` for zero-valued best windows described consistently across the spec, plan, and contract-facing artifacts? [Consistency, Spec §FR-013]
- [x] CHK014 Do the current tasks still map tomorrow-specific behavior to independent work items, rather than implicitly folding tomorrow behavior into today-only acceptance? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-015]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [x] CHK015 Can the success criteria be reviewed objectively from documented state and attribute outcomes without assuming hidden entity internals? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §SC-001, Spec §SC-003, Spec §SC-006]
- [x] CHK016 Is the no-regression requirement measurable enough to distinguish preservation of existing sensor count from preservation of existing sensor behavior? [Measurability, Spec §FR-001a, Spec §SC-007]
- [x] CHK017 Is the tie-break requirement measurable enough that a reviewer could tell whether equal-price ordering is fully specified for both first and second place? [Measurability, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011, Spec §SC-002]

## Scenario Coverage

- [x] CHK018 Are same-day decision support and next-day planning both covered consistently in requirements, plan scope, and task sequencing? [Coverage, Spec §FR-003, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2]
- [x] CHK019 Are degraded-data scenarios covered clearly enough across artifacts to show when only one sensor slice should become unavailable instead of implying a broader feature failure? [Coverage, Spec §FR-014, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK020 Are equal-price scenarios with more than two matching candidates addressed strongly enough that second-place selection remains unambiguous? [Coverage, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-011, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK021 Are zero-best-price scenarios documented as valid ranked outputs with a partial attribute omission, rather than as unavailable-state scenarios? [Coverage, Spec §FR-013, Conflict]

## Dependencies & Assumptions

- [x] CHK022 Are assumptions about coordinator-managed `prices_today` and `prices_tomorrow` payloads documented strongly enough that the feature does not depend on unstated direct entity scraping behavior? [Assumption, Spec §Assumptions]
- [x] CHK023 Is the dependency on translation-backed entity identity explicit enough in the documented requirements set, rather than living only in implementation conventions? [Gap]
- [x] CHK024 Are rounding requirements for prices and percentage expressed as output requirements rather than hidden formatting assumptions? [Clarity, Spec §FR-008, Spec §FR-009, Spec §FR-012]

## Ambiguities & Conflicts

- [x] CHK025 Is there any remaining ambiguity about whether "independence" means separate ranking inputs only, or also separate availability outcomes per sensor slice? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-015]
- [x] CHK026 Do any artifacts still imply that the older midday sensor contract is being redefined by this feature instead of merely preserved alongside it? [Conflict, Spec §FR-001a]
- [x] CHK027 Is the out-of-scope boundary for future buy-window sensors strong enough to prevent them from being smuggled into this feature through later task edits? [Ambiguity, Spec §FR-001b]

## Traceability & Delivery Readiness

- [x] CHK028 Can each user story be traced to at least one distinct task cluster and one measurable outcome without gaps between requirement and delivery planning? [Traceability, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3]
- [x] CHK029 Are cross-cutting regression expectations traceable to both planning constraints and dedicated tasks, rather than being mentioned only as a general warning? [Traceability, Spec §FR-001a, Spec §SC-007]
- [x] CHK030 Does the current artifact set give an author enough clarity to review scope, ranking, degradation, and coexistence without reopening clarification? [Traceability, Spec §FR-001b, Spec §FR-010, Spec §FR-014]

## Notes

- This is a standard-rigor, author-facing checklist focused on consistency between the spec, plan, contract-oriented design, and tasks.
- It intentionally tests requirement quality and cross-artifact alignment, not implementation behavior.
